name: Sun Wan (Qi)
page_title: Sun Wan (Qi) 孫上雨下單 (莔) [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Son of Sun Xiu, and intended heir. Puyang Xiang and Zhang Bu enthroned Sun Hao.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	孫上雨下單 (莔)


	 Simplified Chinese 
	孙上雨下單


	 Pronunciation 
	Sun1 Shang4yu3xia4shan4 (Meng2)


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	SÅ«n Shàngyǔxiàshàn (Méng)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Sun Wan (Mêng)

	 Birthplace 
	Wu

	 Name Notes 
	The character ‘Wan1’ in Sun Wan’s did not exist in Unicode for quite some time, but it is now listed. ’&#168294’ is the character, but odds are you can’t see it. 上雨下單, the name we are currently using, is simply an explanation of the character, which has has 雨 on the top and 單 on the bottom. 上 and 下 mean up and down respectively.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Sun Wan, styled Qi, was from Fuchun county, Wu commandery. He was the oldest son of Sun Xiu. On September 19, 262, Sun Wan was made Crown Prince, accompanied by a grand amnesty. However, Sun Wan would never succeed Sun Xiu. Sun Xiu entrusted Puyang Xing and Zhang Bu to ensure Sun Wan would succeed, even having Sun Wan pay his respects to them, but after Sun Xiu died, they believed Sun Wan to be too young to be ruling Wu so they selected his first cousin Sun Hao instead. In the 10th lunar month of 264, Sun Wan was made Prince of Yuzhang by Sun Hao.

His name is unique to him. A part of an edict by Sun Xiu stated, “The Crown Prince shall be named [Sun] Wan. Wan shall be pronounced inspired by ‘lakes and gulfs’, so Wan. His style name shall be Qi. Qi shall be pronounced inspired by ‘reaching the present’, so Qi.”

full_text:
Sun Wan (Qi) 孫上雨下單 (莔)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sun Wan (孫上雨下單), styledQi (莔), was born in Wu. Son of Sun Xiu, and intended heir. Puyang Xiang and Zhang Bu enthroned Sun Hao. Sun Wan was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Sun Wan, styled Qi, was from Fuchun county, Wu commandery. He was the oldest son of Sun Xiu. On September 19, 262, Sun Wan was made Crown Prince, accompanied by a grand amnesty. However, Sun Wan would never succeed Sun Xiu. Sun Xiu entrusted Puyang Xing and Zhang Bu to ensure Sun Wan would succeed, even having Sun Wan pay his respects to them, but after Sun Xiu died, they believed Sun Wan to be too young to be ruling Wu so they selected his first cousin Sun Hao instead. In the 10th lunar month of 264, Sun Wan was made Prince of Yuzhang by Sun Hao.
His name is unique to him. A part of an edict by Sun Xiu stated, “The Crown Prince shall be named [Sun] Wan. Wan shall be pronounced inspired by ‘lakes and gulfs’, so Wan. His style name shall be Qi. Qi shall be pronounced inspired by ‘reaching the present’, so Qi.”
